basic_details.job_title: UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 196 Posts
basic_details.department: Medical Health and Family Welfare, Uttarakhand
basic_details.short_information: Download UKMSSB Nursing Officer Notification PDF Apply Online Here

application_fee.details: The fee structure for this recruitment is category-based, paid entirely online, and is non-refundable once submitted — even if the candidate later cancels the application. Below is the exact amount payable by category. | Ex-Servicemen from Uttarakhand pay the fee applicable to their own social category (General/SC/ST/OBC), not a separate concessional rate. Payment can be made via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI, and an application without completed payment will be rejected outright.

age_limit.details: The age cut-off date for this recruitment is fixed at 1 July 2025 , not the application closing date. Candidates must be at least 21 years old and not older than 42 years as on that date, with relaxation available to specific reserved categories domiciled in Uttarakhand. | Category Relaxation SC / ST / OBC (Uttarakhand) 5 years above maximum age PwBD (Differently Abled) 10 years above maximum age Ex-Servicemen (Uttarakhand) Military service period deducted; resultant age capped at 45 years Dependents of Freedom Fighters (Uttarakhand) 5 years Uttarakhand State Orphans As per claimed vertical category EWS No relaxation | Only one age relaxation benefit applies per candidate, even if eligible for multiple categories , so candidates should claim the one most favourable to them rather than expecting cumulative relaxations.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering if they qualify for this recruitment can note that eligibility depends on three things — nursing qualification, council registration, and Uttarakhand domicile (for reservation benefits). The sections below break this down post code by post code. | All four post codes accept candidates holding any one of the following nursing qualifications from an Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognised institution: B.Sc. (Honours) in Nursing, B.Sc. Nursing (regular course), Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing, or Diploma in General Nursing and Midwifery/Psychiatric Nursing. Whether a candidate's qualification falls under "diploma holder" or "degree holder" post codes depends on the nature of their original nursing programme, so candidates should match their qualification carefully against the official notification before selecting a post code. | In addition to the nursing qualification, every applicant must have working knowledge of Hindi and a valid, renewed permanent registration certificate from the Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council, Dehradun, issued on or before 2 July 2026. Importantly, an NUI card or a mere receipt of registration application is not accepted — only the permanent registration certificate qualifies. A desirable (optional) qualification of either 2 years of Territorial Army service or an NCC 'B' or 'C' certificate can strengthen an application but is not mandatory. | The age cut-off date for this recruitment is fixed at 1 July 2025, not the application closing date. Candidates must be at least 21 years old and not older than 42 years as on that date, with relaxation available to specific reserved categories domiciled in Uttarakhand. | Only one age relaxation benefit applies per candidate, even if eligible for multiple categories, so candidates should claim the one most favourable to them rather than expecting cumulative relaxations. | Reservation benefits — both vertical (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) and horizontal (PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Freedom Fighter Dependents, Orphans, State Agitators, Skilled Sportspersons) — are available only to permanent residents of Uttarakhand. Candidates from outside Uttarakhand belonging to a reserved category elsewhere will be treated as unreserved here unless they hold a valid Uttarakhand domicile certificate. OBC candidates must additionally ensure their certificate is dated no earlier than 2 July 2023 (within 3 years of the application deadline) and excludes them from the creamy layer, while EWS candidates need an Income and Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about pay can note that all four post codes carry an identical salary structure, since the post grade does not vary by gender or qualification type. Selected Nursing Officers will be placed at Pay Level 7 of the 7th Pay Commission, drawing Rs. 44,900 to Rs. 1,42,400 per month, with pay fixed as per prevailing government rules and a contributory pension applicable to the post.

selection_process: Selection under this recruitment follows the Uttarakhand Subordinate Nursing (Non-Gazetted) Service Rules

and is based entirely on a written competitive examination — there is no interview stage. Shortlisted candidates then proceed to document verification and a medical examination before final appointment.
The exam has two papers of 100 marks each
with the nursing subject paper content varying slightly between diploma and degree holders.
General Knowledge & General Studies (100 Marks): General Hindi (20 marks) covers grammar
and idioms; General Knowledge (40 marks) covers Uttarakhand and national/international geography
and current affairs; General Studies (40 marks) tests reasoning
Negative marking: 1/4 mark deducted per wrong answer; unattempted questions carry no penalty. Qualifying marks: 45% for General/OBC/EWS candidates
35% for SC/ST candidates. Answer sheet: Triplicate OMR — original and second copy go to the invigilator
the carbon copy can be taken home by the candidate. Question booklet: Bilingual (Hindi & English)
and candidates may keep it after the exam. Answer key objections: Published on www.ukmssb.org; objections accepted within 7 days at Rs. 50 per question. Exam centres: Dehradun
Almora — candidates choose two preferred cities
though the Board may reassign based on numbers. Prohibited items: Calculators
and any communication device. Tie-breaker: Higher desirable qualification first
Candidates appearing for the exam can also claim a 50% bus fare concession on Uttarakhand Transport Corporation buses
valid two days before and two days after the exam
on producing the admit card and domicile certificate copy to the conductor.
how_to_apply: UKMSSB accepts applications exclusively through its website, with no offline submission allowed. Following these five steps in order helps candidates avoid the most common application errors.

Register: Go to www.ukmssb.org → click "Apply Now" → "Candidate Register Here." Complete registration with a valid email ID and mobile number, and select the correct post code carefully at this stage, since it cannot be changed later.

Log In and Fill the Form: Use the Registration Number and Password sent to your email/mobile to log in, then enter personal details, educational qualification, and council registration details (registration number, date, validity). Select two preferred exam centre cities from Dehradun, Srinagar, Haldwani, or Almora.
Upload Documents: Upload a passport photo (20-50 KB, JPG) and signature (5-20 KB, JPG), plus a single combined PDF (200 KB - 2 MB) containing all mandatory and applicable certificates.
Pay the Fee: Pay the category-applicable fee online via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI, and save the receipt — applications without fee payment will be rejected.
Download and Save: After successful payment, download and print the final application form for future reference.

A one-time correction window opens from 4 July to 7 July 2026 (5:00 PM), where candidates can log in with their Registration Number and Password to fix most details — except mobile number and email ID. If a candidate changes category or sub-category during this window, any extra fee already paid for the earlier category will not be refunded.

faq.question: Q1. What is the UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification date?
faq.answer: The Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB) released the Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification on 03-06-2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many Nursing Officer vacancies are available under UKMSSB 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 196 Nursing Officer posts, including male and female candidates with diploma and degree qualifications.

faq.question: Q3. What are the eligibility criteria for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess GNM, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, B.Sc. Nursing, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing degrees.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants should be between 21 and 42 years. Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: General/OBC: Rs. 300 SC/ST/EWS/PWD: Rs. 150 Payment is to be made online.

faq.question: Q6. What is the UKMSSB Nursing Officer pay scale 2026?
faq.answer: The salary is Rs. 44,900 – Rs. 1,42,400 (Level-7) as per the 7th CPC pay matrix.

faq.question: Q7. How can I apply online for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply through the official UKMSSB website: ukmssb.org. Ensure eligibility before submitting the application.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I download the UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be downloaded directly from UKMSSB official website under the Recruitment section.
